scope_boundary: Only the bounded repository-infrastructure claim that the Phase 5 harness registry can be replayed by one local runner command, executing all registered synthetic harnesses and matching each registry result summary, moves to synthetic-test-backed.
transition_reason: The local replay command loaded experiments/phase5_harness_registry.json, executed all 21 registered harness commands, and verified that every output contained its registry result_summary. The registry covers 60 valid synthetic fixtures and 102 expected-invalid synthetic fixtures across the registered harness directories. This is enough to support the narrow repository-infrastructure claim that the harness registry is replayable and summary-checked; it is not evidence for any broader ASI Stack runtime, model, safety, benchmark, or chapter-core claim.
verification_command: python3 scripts/run_phase5_harnesses.py --write-report && python3 scripts/validate_book.py && python3 scripts/validate_evidence_transitions.py
negative_results: expected-invalid fixture sets are present across all 21 registered harness lanes
the replay validates registry-driven synthetic harness execution only
no chapter core claim is promoted above argument
no model, runtime, benchmark, source-interpretation, or deployed-safety result is claimed
downgrade_triggers: registered harness output missing its expected result_summary
missing valid or expected-invalid fixtures for a registered harness
chapter prose interpreting this transition as AI runtime or safety evidence
promotion_burden: Broader claims require a separate accepted transition with public-safe prototype traces, measured cost-quality/resource-budget records, context-admission replay outputs, compression measurements, planner/runtime adapter traces, Theseus transfer evidence, Circle receipt replay, independent review where relevant, and explicit non-claims.
limitations: synthetic fixtures rather than deployed runtime behavior
summary matching verifies expected output text, not semantic completeness of every harness
does not measure model quality, benchmark quality, useful-solution performance, or safety outcomes
does not promote any chapter core claim above argument
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent external audit
non_claims: does not promote any chapter core claim
does not prove deployed runtime behavior
does not prove model quality, benchmark quality, or useful-solution performance
does not prove AI safety, alignment, governance effectiveness, or source interpretation
does not replace future costed-route, context-admission, compression, planner/runtime, Theseus, or Circle evidence slices
